stocks.news: Adobe said CEO Shantanu Narayen will step down after the company has put in place a successor. Narayen, who has helmed the design software maker since 2007, will stay on as board chair. Adobe shares are down 23% this year as part of a broader selloff in software sparked by concerns about the impact of artificial intelligence.

economy.commentary:
Crude Oil WTI fell sharply to $92.74, down $4.10 (-4.23%), as global markets reacted to shifting supply dynamics.

The University of Michigan's Consumer Sentiment Index fell to 55.5 in March, its lowest reading of 2026. Interviews taken before the U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran showed improving sentiment — those taken after completely erased the gains.

The index is now sitting in the 2nd percentile of its entire recorded history, below the level seen at the start of every recession since 1978.

crypto.commentary:
BlackRock's new ether ETF for yield-hungry investors debuts with $15 million in trading volume, signaling growing institutional appetite for Ethereum-based products.

Bitcoin is holding steady near $73,700 while Ethereum trades at $2,018.92. XRP continues to draw attention from strategic-focused investors at $1.40.

real_estate.homebuilder: U.S. homebuilder sentiment edged up to 38 in March, beating expectations, though sentiment remains below the 50 break-even point for the 23rd straight month. PulteGroup also announced the nomination of Benjamin Schall, CEO of AvalonBay Communities, to its Board of Directors.
real_estate.commentary:
The Bay Area market is entering a "steady but not overheating" phase. While median prices remain high at roughly $1.25M region-wide, inventory is projected to rise by 10% this year, helping to ease the intense bidding wars of previous years.

The lock-in effect is still the biggest drag on housing supply. Millions of homeowners who refinanced at 3% during the pandemic have no incentive to sell — they'd have to take on a new mortgage at roughly double that rate. Most economists think rates would need to fall closer to 5% before that logjam meaningfully breaks.

At 6%, buying a home is more affordable than it was a year ago. But the window that was quietly opening over the past few months just got a little narrower.

Latest Real Estate News in the Bay Area:

📍 Santa Clara County: The market is a notable outlier with a 3.5% year-over-year price decline, creating rare negotiating room for buyers that doesn't exist in neighboring San Francisco.

📍 Alameda County: Remains the most affordable entry point in the region with a median price of $925K, though competition is heating up in Fremont and Oakland as buyers re-enter the market to beat the peak April/May rush.

📍 Geopolitical Impact: Rising tensions in the Middle East are causing "economic jitters" among some California buyers, with rising gas prices starting to impact consumer sentiment and home-purchasing decisions.

Latest General News in the Bay Area:

📍 Fremont — "Happiest City" Again: Fremont has officially been named the "Happiest City in America" for the sixth consecutive year, leading the nation with the highest percentage of households earning at least $75,000 and the lowest divorce rate.

📍 Fremont Shelter Crisis: In response to the regional housing crisis, the City of Fremont adopted a Shelter Crisis Declaration late last week to streamline local support services.

📍 Milpitas — AI Politics: Local leaders are participating in the launch of a new AI-driven political campaign platform, VotersAI, while community organizations continue to focus on public safety and school facility improvements.
